summary:
Neonatal central cyanosis combined with a continuous systolic murmur strongly suggests ventricular septal defect (VSD), which causes right-to-left shunt leading to this presentation. Therefore, the correct answer remains \( \boxed{D} \).

The summary focuses on highlighting how both signs point towards VSD based on pathophysiology matching exactly what was observed clinically without extraneous details that do not contribute directly
